text.title: AI-Enabled Business Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every AI-Enabled Business Analysis application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Perform data quality assessment before requirements elicitation on every AI initiative.

rationale.source: AI-Enabled Business Analysis authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: 60% of AI project failures trace to poor data quality; the BA gate is the highest-leverage early activity.

rule.statement: NLP-extracted requirements are first drafts, never final; a human BA reviews every record.

rationale.source: AI-Enabled Business Analysis authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: NLP loses implicit knowledge; treating drafts as final ships hallucinated requirements.

rule.statement: Each requirement names a stakeholder source with role + handle.

rationale.source: AI-Enabled Business Analysis authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Anonymous requirements lose provenance; named stakeholder enables clarification.

rule.statement: AI initiative classified per AI TRiSM (trust / risk / security / management) before requirements freeze.

rationale.source: AI-Enabled Business Analysis authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: TRiSM mis-classification creates legal exposure and slows downstream review.

rule.statement: For agentic systems, the BA spec names agent boundaries, escalation rules, and human-oversight requirements.

rationale.source: AI-Enabled Business Analysis authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Missing boundaries cause runaway agents and unbounded blast radius.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.
